title: andropogon scoparius subsp. euscoparius
intro: Andropogon scoparius subsp. euscoparius, also known as broom sedge or little bluestem, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Andropogon scoparius subsp. euscoparius include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic, making it a valuable herb for supporting overall health and well-being. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and crushing the leaves and stems of the plant.
